text: Refer URGENTLY to hospital. Give any pre-referral treatment.
Danger sign: child has or has had convulsions.
Danger sign: child is not able to drink or breastfeed.
source_quote: Is the child not able to drink or breastfeed?

text: Danger sign: child vomits everything.
Danger sign: child is unusually sleepy or unconscious.
source_quote: Is the child unusually sleepy or unconscious?

text: Classification: PNEUMONIA. Cough with fast breathing.
Classification: SEVERE PNEUMONIA. Cough with chest indrawing. Refer urgently.
Classification: COUGH OR COLD. No fast breathing, no chest indrawing.
Give oral amoxicillin twice daily for 5 days.
Advise: soothe the throat with safe remedy. Continue feeding. Return if breathing becomes difficult or fast, or child becomes sicker.
source_quote: Advise the caretaker on home care

text: Follow up in 3 days. If still fast breathing, refer to hospital.
source_quote: Follow up in 3 days for cough with fast breathing

text: Classification: MALARIA. RDT positive.
Classification: FEVER, NO MALARIA. RDT negative.
Give AL (Artemether-Lumefantrine) twice daily for 3 days.
Fever for 7 days or more. Refer to hospital.
source_quote: If fever for 7 days or more, refer

text: Classification: DIARRHOEA. Give ORS and zinc.
source_quote: If diarrhoea: give ORS and zinc

text: Classification: DYSENTERY. Blood in stool. Refer.
Give ORS after each loose stool. Show caretaker how to prepare and give ORS.
Give zinc supplement once daily for 10 days. Under 6 months: half tablet; 6 months and older: 1 tablet.
Follow up in 5 days. If diarrhoea not improved, refer.
source_quote: Follow up in 5 days for diarrhoea

text: Classification: SEVERE ACUTE MALNUTRITION. MUAC red or oedema of both feet. Refer urgently.
source_quote: Red on MUAC tape or oedema of both feet: refer urgently

text: Classification: MODERATE ACUTE MALNUTRITION. MUAC yellow. Refer for supplementary feeding.
Advise caretaker to give more fluids and continue feeding.
source_quote: Give more fluids and continue feeding

text: Return immediately if child: cannot drink or breastfeed, becomes sicker, has blood in stool, develops fever.
